name: x402-metered-api-acceptance
description: Acceptance contract for the x402 keyless metered API demo. Burner path is executed end-to-end when a Test Signer is provided; HashPack/WalletConnect is affordance + architecture only (not automated).

evaluationRules.failOnUncertainty: If an assertion cannot be positively verified from the running app, mark it FAILED, not passed. Absence of evidence is a failure.
evaluationRules.credentials: The app must load, render, and be browsable without any .env file, private keys, operator keys, or funded testnet account. Missing credentials must not excuse a broken read path.
evaluationRules.walletGatedFlows: When the harness provides a Test Signer in the validator prompt: assertions flagged executableWithTestSigner=true MUST be executed end-to-end via the burner wallet (inject burnerWallet.pk, connect Burner, complete flow, verify on-chain via mirror node). Other walletRequired assertions without that flag remain affordance-only. HashPack / Hedera WalletConnect must be present as a connect option and documented, but MUST NOT be required to complete a live HashPack signature in headless validation. When no Test Signer is provided, treat all walletRequired assertions as affordance-only.
evaluationRules.onChainVerification: For executableWithTestSigner assertions, confirm effects via the Hedera testnet mirror node REST API (https://testnet.mirrornode.hedera.com). Poll up to ~30s for mirror lag. Cite mirror evidence in the verdict.
evaluationRules.hybridSigner: Native Hedera actions (x402 partial-sign and HCS execute) must go through a dual-path signer: BurnerKeySigner when burnerWallet.pk / burner connector is active; WalletConnectHederaSigner when HashPack/Hedera WC is active. x402 must use sign-only (never wallet submit). EVM-only wallets without a burner key must surface a clear error, not a silent failure.
evaluationRules.evidence: Every verdict issue must cite what was observed (route visited, element found/absent, console output, API responses, mirror node responses for on-chain flows).

severityMeaning.critical: Route/app is broken or a core journey is impossible; run cannot pass.
severityMeaning.major: A required PRD capability is missing or non-functional on the read/affordance level.
severityMeaning.minor: Coherence, clarity, or documentation gap that degrades the demo but does not break a journey.

assertions.statement: The home route renders without runtime/console errors and shows the metered API demo concept.
assertions.howToVerify: Navigate to /. Confirm the page loads, primary content about x402/paid API is visible, and the console shows no uncaught errors or crash banners.

assertions.statement: The home page identifies the Hedera network (testnet) and shows which facilitator is in use.
assertions.howToVerify: On /, look for visible indication of Testnet and mention of the x402 facilitator or payment scheme.

assertions.statement: A free API endpoint is callable without payment or wallet and returns valid JSON data.
assertions.howToVerify: From the UI, trigger the free endpoint call (button or auto-fetch). Confirm a JSON response is displayed or accessible. Alternatively, call /api/data/free (or equivalent) directly and confirm HTTP 200 with a JSON body.

assertions.statement: The paid API endpoint returns HTTP 402 Payment Required with valid x402 PaymentRequirements when called without payment.
assertions.howToVerify: Call the paid endpoint (e.g. /api/data/paid) without an X-PAYMENT header. Confirm the response is HTTP 402 and the body or headers contain PaymentRequirements with at least: scheme, network, amount, asset, payTo.

assertions.statement: The home page UI clearly shows the price and payment requirement for the paid endpoint before any wallet action.
assertions.howToVerify: On /, confirm the paid endpoint section visually displays the cost (e.g. HBAR amount) and indicates a payment is required to access it.

assertions.statement: A wallet-connect affordance is available and offers both Burner (dev) and HashPack / Hedera WalletConnect paths.
assertions.howToVerify: On /, open Connect Wallet (or equivalent). Confirm Burner Wallet (or Development burner) is available for harness/demo use, and a HashPack or Hedera WalletConnect option is available for real users. Presence in the modal/UI is enough; do not complete a HashPack signature.

assertions.statement: Connecting the burner wallet with the harness test signer key succeeds and the app shows a connected account in Burner mode.
assertions.howToVerify: Inject the test signer private key into localStorage (burnerWallet.pk), reload, open Connect Wallet, choose Burner Wallet. Confirm the header shows a connected account and the UI indicates burner/dev signer mode when shown.

assertions.statement: With the burner wallet connected, triggering the paid endpoint completes the x402 payment flow and returns premium data (HTTP 200).
assertions.howToVerify: With burner connected and funded, trigger the paid API call from the UI. The app should handle 402 → partial-sign via burner key → retry automatically. Confirm the UI shows success with the premium JSON data. Verify via mirror node that a transfer transaction from the test signer account exists (GET /api/v1/transactions?account.id=SIGNER&order=desc&limit=5). The transfer should send the advertised amount to the payTo account.

assertions.statement: After a successful paid call, the UI shows a HashScan link or settlement transaction ID so the user can verify on-chain.
assertions.howToVerify: After C8 succeeds, confirm the UI surfaces a transaction ID or a link to HashScan (testnet) for the settlement.

assertions.statement: After a successful payment, a receipt message appears in the HCS topic and is visible in the receipt feed on the home page.
assertions.howToVerify: After C8 settles, wait up to 30s and check: (1) the receipt feed on / shows the new entry with payer/amount/endpoint info, and (2) mirror node GET /api/v1/topics/{topicId}/messages confirms a receipt message containing the settlement tx ID or payer account.

assertions.statement: The /admin route renders without runtime/console errors.
assertions.howToVerify: Navigate to /admin. Confirm the page loads and the console shows no uncaught errors.

assertions.statement: The admin page shows facilitator configuration (URL, fee-payer) and the current payTo account and price.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, confirm visible display of the facilitator URL (or its name), the payTo account ID, and the per-call price.

assertions.statement: The admin page exposes a control to create an HCS receipt topic.
assertions.howToVerify: On /admin, locate a labeled control for creating an HCS topic for receipts.

assertions.statement: README and/or AGENTS.md document the dual signer model: burner for harness/demos and HashPack via Hedera WalletConnect for real users, including that x402 uses sign-only and HCS uses sign-and-execute.
assertions.howToVerify: Read README.md and AGENTS.md in the workspace. Confirm both signer paths are described and the x402 vs HCS wallet method distinction is stated (signTransaction / partial-sign vs signAndExecute).

assertions.statement: Source code implements a dual-path Hedera signer (burner private-key path and WalletConnect/HashPack path) rather than reading burnerWallet.pk for all native actions.
assertions.howToVerify: Inspect the packages/nextjs source for a signer abstraction or clearly separated Burner vs WalletConnect signing helpers. Confirm paid/HCS flows do not exclusively depend on localStorage burnerWallet.pk when a HashPack session is the intended path. Presence of Hedera WalletConnect / HashPack integration code is required.

assertions.statement: The full app boots and all routes are reachable with no .env, private keys, or funded account present.
assertions.howToVerify: With no credentials configured, visit / and /admin. All must render their intended UI. Any route that hard-fails due to missing credentials is a failure.

assertions.statement: The demo surfaces no Solidity/contract-deploy or local-chain tooling flows.
assertions.howToVerify: Across the routes, confirm there are no Hardhat, Foundry, or Docker deploy steps presented to the user.
